headline: Semis Surge on TSMC, Samsung Investments; Fed Eyes AI Inflation; Trump's Crypto Windfall
summary: The semiconductor sector saw significant gains as TSMC accelerated advanced packaging and 2nm development, with Samsung and SK Hynix planning major investments. Fed officials signaled potential rate hikes due to AI-fueled inflation concerns, while oil prices reacted to US-Iran tensions. Donald Trump reported over $1.4 billion in crypto earnings post-presidency.

bullets.lead: TSMC is accelerating advanced packaging and 2nm process development.

segments.text: TSMC is fast-tracking CoPoS technology, with the entire supply chain under a gag order, and its 2nm, CoWoS, and CoPoS advancements are broadly benefiting Taiwan's equipment, materials, and packaging suppliers.

sources.title: TSMC fast-tracks CoPoS—whole supply chain under gag order

segments.text: TSMC's Q2 gross margin is expected to approach 70%, exceeding guidance, with Q3 revenue projected to increase 10% sequentially, and full-year 2026 revenue guidance is also anticipated to be strong.

bullets.lead: Major chip-tool stocks are rising on significant investment plans from Samsung and SK.

segments.text: ASML and other chip-tool stocks are soaring as Samsung and SK Hynix announce substantial investments in the sector.

sources.title: ASML, Chip-Tool Stocks Soar as Samsung, SK Plan Investments

segments.text: The Wafer Fab Equipment (WFE) sector is demonstrating strong performance, with demand for DUVi, etch, and deposition tools expected to drive significant growth in WFE sub-systems.

bullets.lead: Corning's glass substrate thesis is playing out with strong performance.

segments.text: Corning (GLW) is exhibiting significant strength, with its glass substrate thesis being highlighted by analysts, reinforcing confidence in its performance.

bullets.lead: Memory suppliers are securing long-term agreements with customers.

segments.text: Major memory suppliers including Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron, Kioxia, and SanDisk are entering into 3-5 year Long Term Agreements (LTAs) with customers, often incorporating price floors and ceilings.

bullets.lead: China's CXMT is expanding its presence in the memory market.

segments.text: China's CXMT has secured a $3 billion memory supply deal with Tencent and is in discussions with other Chinese firms, though analysts believe Apple is unlikely to source DRAM from CXMT.

bullets.lead: Apple is navigating challenges from rising memory prices.

segments.text: Apple is reportedly facing difficulties due to increasing memory prices, yet simultaneously, it may gain global market share as a result of these same price hikes, indicating a complex market dynamic.

bullets.lead: Fed officials are signaling potential rate hikes due to AI-fueled inflation.

segments.text: Cleveland Fed President Hammack indicated that AI advancements could contribute to inflation, suggesting that further rate hikes might be necessary.

sources.title: Cleveland Fed President Hammack says AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may be necessary

bullets.lead: Oil prices are reacting to US-Iran tensions and US production data.

segments.text: Oil prices rose as the U.S. and Iran discussed a deal to suspend Gulf attacks, while U.S. oil production reached a record high in April.

sources.title: US oil production rises to record high in April, EIA says
Oil Prices Rise as U.S. and Iran Discuss Deal to Suspend Gulf Attacks

segments.text: The U.S. Treasury Secretary warned oil and gas companies to lower prices, stating the government is "watching" their actions.

sources.title: US treasury secretary warns oil and gas companies to lower prices: ‘We’re watching’

bullets.lead: Gold prices are holding steady amidst geopolitical tensions impacting inflation outlook.

segments.text: Gold held its drop as US-Iran tensions weighed on the inflation outlook, reflecting market sensitivity to geopolitical events.

sources.title: Gold Holds Drop as US-Iran Tensions Weigh on Inflation Outlook

bullets.lead: Donald Trump reported substantial crypto earnings after leaving office.

segments.text: Donald Trump's annual disclosures revealed at least $1.4 billion in crypto earnings in 2025, including over $500 million from the sale of family crypto tokens and $236 million in crypto proceeds.

sources.title: Trump Had a Billion-Dollar Windfall After Returning to the White House
Trump made more than $500mn from sale of family’s crypto tokens last year
Trump Reports at Least $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings

bullets.lead: Trump's Supreme Court successes face economic challenges.

segments.text: Despite wins at the Supreme Court, the economy remains a significant hurdle for Trump.

sources.title: Trump's Supreme Court wins hit one wall: the economy

bullets.lead: The World Bank plans to phase out lending to China.

segments.text: The World Bank announced its intention to gradually discontinue lending to China.

sources.title: World Bank to phase out China lending

bullets.lead: Anthropic is expanding its AI agent capabilities and renegotiating pricing with Amazon.

segments.text: Anthropic is preparing to integrate its Claude agent into Microsoft Teams, while also renegotiating Claude usage pricing with Amazon from compute hours to tokens, indicating a strained relationship following Amazon's substantial investment.

sources.title: Anthropic Preps Claude Agent in Microsoft Teams

bullets.lead: Google's AI boom is significantly increasing its emissions and power consumption.

segments.text: Google's rapid expansion in AI is leading to a substantial rise in its emissions and power usage.

sources.title: Google's AI boom sends emissions, power use soaring

bullets.lead: Meta is facing legal challenges regarding the addictive nature of its platforms for children.

segments.text: Meta lost its bid to dismiss claims from US states alleging that Facebook and Instagram addict children.

sources.title: Meta loses bid to dismiss US states' claims that Facebook, Instagram addict children

bullets.lead: Nvidia rallied on strong guidance, boosting the semiconductor index.

segments.text: Nvidia's stock rallied following a guidance beat, contributing to an 88% surge in the semiconductor index during Q2, despite geopolitical oil price volatility.

segments.text: Nike posted earnings that exceeded expectations in its latest quarter, though sales continued to decline, partly due to weakness in China.

segments.text: Anthropic launched Claude Science as part of its strategy to generate revenue from the pharmaceutical industry.

x_themes.summary: Memory suppliers like Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron, Kioxia, and SanDisk are signing 3-5 year Long Term Agreements (LTAs) with customers, often including price floors, ceilings, and upfront payments, indicating a structural shift towards sustained high margins in the memory business. Bernstein raised SanDisk's PT to $3000 based on these new LTA terms.

x_themes.topic: Humanoid Robotics Market and Supply Chain
x_themes.summary: The humanoid robotics sector is gaining validation, with companies like Agility Robotics securing large orders and General Motors replacing workers with robots. While Korea aims to lead the non-China supply chain, investment opportunities are challenging due to private companies or diluted exposure within conglomerates.

x_themes.topic: CXMT's Role in Memory Market and Impact on Micron
x_themes.summary: China's CXMT secured a $3B memory supply deal with Tencent and is in talks with other Chinese firms. Analysts believe it's unlikely Apple will purchase DRAM from CXMT, as the Chinese government would likely prioritize domestic demand, and CXMT's capacity is insufficient even for local needs, posing no immediate threat to Micron.

TSMC's Q2 gross margin is expected to near 70%, surpassing guidance, with Q3 revenue projected to rise 10% sequentially. The company's full-year 2026 revenue guidance is also anticipated to be raised from over 30%.

Due to ongoing memory shortages, TSMC is collaborating with Winbond Electronics for DRAM memory wafers and Wafer-on-Wafer (WoW) advanced 3D stacking. WoW is a critical AI technology for improving bandwidth, latency, and power efficiency by stacking memory on logic chips.

TSMC has partnered with ShunSin Technology, a Foxconn subsidiary, for Fiber Array Unit (FAU) and Grating Coupling work on its COUPE optical engine platform. ShunSin anticipates double-digit revenue growth and is developing advanced CPO and NPO solutions.
The Wafer Fab Equipment (WFE) sector is showing strong performance, with demand for DUVi, etch, and deposition tools expected to drive significant growth in WFE sub-systems over the next two years.
Corning (GLW) is showing significant strength, with its glass substrate thesis playing out. The company's performance is being highlighted by analysts, reinforcing confidence in its market position.

x_themes.topic: Rising Memory Prices Squeezing Other Semi/Hardware Margins
x_themes.summary: Rising memory prices have begun to squeeze the margins of other semiconductor and hardware names, including AI beneficiaries, a trend that has spread beyond consumer electronics to impact the broader AI, semi, and hardware sectors.

x_themes.topic: Memory Price Hikes and Impact on Apple
x_themes.summary: Apple is reportedly facing challenges due to rising memory prices, but simultaneously, it may gain global market share as a result of these same price hikes, indicating a complex and mixed impact.

x_themes.topic: US Frontier AI Models Maintain Edge over Chinese
x_themes.summary: Leading U.S. frontier AI models maintain a clear edge over their Chinese counterparts in quality and intelligence. Enterprises are unlikely to ditch established models like OpenAI and Anthropic for Chinese alternatives due to scale and compute limitations.

The relationship between Anthropic and Amazon appears strained, with Anthropic renegotiating Claude usage pricing from compute hours to tokens. This follows Amazon's substantial investment and Anthropic's Trainium commitment, potentially leading Amazon to reduce Claude reliance.

x_themes.topic: GLM 5.2 Performance and Open-Source AI Model Competition
x_themes.summary: GLM 5.2 is performing comparably to frontier models like Opus 4.6-4.8, with the gap compressing as third-party token routers enable open-source labs to acquire RL datasets. This makes GLM 5.2 "good enough for the agent workload" for a widening set of tasks.

x_themes.topic: BlackBerry QNX Software Model and Expansion
x_themes.summary: BlackBerry's QNX software-only model is benefiting from high margins despite HBM cost pressures, as Nvidia and Qualcomm standardize vehicle compute platforms on QNX. The company is also expanding QNX into robotics, medical devices, and industrial automation.
